  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Officer",
  "description": "THC Pharmaceuticals is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking for a friendly and customer-focused individual to join our sales company as a Customer Service Officer.
	The ideal candidate will handle customer inquiries, resolve complaints, and support the sales team to ensure excellent customer satisfaction and smooth operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Respond promptly to customer inquiries via phone, email, or in person.
	Resolve customer complaints and provide appropriate solutions.
	Support the sales team with order processing and client follow-ups.
	Maintain accurate records of customer interactions.
	Provide information about company products and services.


Requirements


	HND / B.Sc in any field
	1&ndash;2 years of experience in customer service, preferably in pharmaceutical or medical sales environment.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.
	Proficient in Microsoft Excel, Word, and other applications.
	Ability to multitask, prioritize, and work under pressure.
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy in documentation.
	Professionalism and ability to interact effectively with healthcare professionals.
	Knowledge of pharmaceutical products, healthcare systems, or medical terminology is an added advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: thcrecruitmentlagos@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Service Officer Job at THC Pharmaceuticals




        
    

        
        THC Pharmaceuticals is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking for a friendly and customer-focused individual to join our sales company as a Customer Service Officer.
	The ideal candidate will handle customer inquiries, resolve complaints, and support the sales team to ensure excellent customer satisfaction and smooth operations.


Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Respond promptly to customer inquiries via phone, email, or in person.
	Resolve customer complaints and provide appropriate solutions.
	Support the sales team with order processing and client follow-ups.
	Maintain accurate records of customer interactions.
	Provide information about company products and services.


Requirements


	HND / B.Sc in any field
	1–2 years of experience in customer service, preferably in pharmaceutical or medical sales environment.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.
	Proficient in Microsoft Excel, Word, and other applications.
	Ability to multitask, prioritize, and work under pressure.
	Attention to detail and high level of accuracy in documentation.
	Professionalism and ability to interact effectively with healthcare professionals.
	Knowledge of pharmaceutical products, healthcare systems, or medical terminology is an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Optometrist",
  "description": "Soteria Eye Clinic is a private enterprise registered in Nigeria as a limited liability company to offer high quality, affordable and accessible comprehensive world class eye care services to our immediate community and environs. Our purpose is to create and deliver first class comprehensive eye care services to ALL in order to &ldquo;Improve Lives through Affordable Quality Eye Care.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Optometrist

Job Locations: Lekki - Lagos and Uyo - Akwa Ibom
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Conduct comprehensive eye examinations using appropriate diagnostic equipment.
	Diagnose refractive errors and prescribe eyeglasses and contact lenses.
	Detect, diagnose, and manage common eye diseases and visual disorders.
	Identify patients requiring specialist ophthalmic care and make appropriate referrals.
	Interpret diagnostic test results, including visual field tests, OCT scans, and retinal imaging.
	Prescribe medications within the scope of professional practice.
	Educate patients on eye health, preventive care, and treatment plans.
	Maintain accurate and confidential patient medical records.
	Follow up with patients to monitor treatment outcomes and ensure continuity of care.
	Collaborate with ophthalmologists, nurses, opticians, and other healthcare professionals to provide holistic patient care.
	Ensure compliance with clinical protocols, infection prevention standards, and regulatory requirements.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment20207@gmail.com or hrofficer@soteriaeyeclinic.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Optometrist Job at Soteria Eye Clinic




        
    

        
        Soteria Eye Clinic is a private enterprise registered in Nigeria as a limited liability company to offer high quality, affordable and accessible comprehensive world class eye care services to our immediate community and environs. Our purpose is to create and deliver first class comprehensive eye care services to ALL in order to “Improve Lives through Affordable Quality Eye Care.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Optometrist

Job Locations: Lekki - Lagos and Uyo - Akwa Ibom
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Conduct comprehensive eye examinations using appropriate diagnostic equipment.
	Diagnose refractive errors and prescribe eyeglasses and contact lenses.
	Detect, diagnose, and manage common eye diseases and visual disorders.
	Identify patients requiring specialist ophthalmic care and make appropriate referrals.
	Interpret diagnostic test results, including visual field tests, OCT scans, and retinal imaging.
	Prescribe medications within the scope of professional practice.
	Educate patients on eye health, preventive care, and treatment plans.
	Maintain accurate and confidential patient medical records.
	Follow up with patients to monitor treatment outcomes and ensure continuity of care.
	Collaborate with ophthalmologists, nurses, opticians, and other healthcare professionals to provide holistic patient care.
	Ensure compliance with clinical protocols, infection prevention standards, and regulatory requirements.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] or [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Control Officer",
  "description": "Bukka Hut is a popular Nigerian quick-service restaurant brand known for serving local Nigerian meals in a clean, modern, and comfortable environment. The company started in 2011 and has grown into one of Lagos&rsquo; best-known indigenous food chains. The brand combines traditional &ldquo;buka&rdquo; style Nigerian food with modern restaurant service and hygiene standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Officer

Job Location: Ogudu, Lagos

Role Summary


	The Quality Control Officer (QC) is responsible for ensuring that all food products prepared in the Central Kitchen and Pastry meet Bukka Hut&rsquo;s quality, safety, hygiene, and consistency standards before dispatch to outlets.
	They support operational excellence by monitoring processes, validating CCPs, enforcing SOP compliance, and preventing quality defects.


Core Duties&nbsp;


	Inspect all incoming raw materials for quality, freshness, packaging integrity, and compliance according to SOP.
	Verify supplier documents (COA, shelf life, specifications).
	Monitor all production stages to ensure SOP and CCP compliance.
	Check recipe measurements, cooking times, temperatures, and portion accuracy.
	Ensure proper hygiene practices for staff, equipment, utensils, and workstations.
	Enforce food safety standards and HACCP guidelines.
	Conduct organoleptic analysis ( taste, texture, colour, and appearance) checks on finished products
	Approve or reject finished batches before dispatch.
	Verify packaging, labelling, batch coding, and storage conditions.
	Maintain accurate quality logs, records, and CCP documentation.
	Report non-conformances and follow up on corrective actions.
	Check equipment calibration (thermometers, weighing scales, etc).
	Ensure proper sanitation, cleaning schedules, and waste management.
	Support and train CK staff on hygiene and quality requirements.
	Identify and escalate quality risks or deviations immediately.
	Recommend process or recipe improvements where needed.
	Monitor pest control and environmental conditions.
	Validate dispatch conditions (temperature, packaging, cold chain).
	Ensure only approved products are released to outlets.
	Support audits, internal inspections, and quality reviews.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Reduction in non-conforming materials
	First-time quality pass rate
	Compliance with HACCP and SOP requirements
	Reduction in outlet complaints linked to CK
	Documentation accuracy and timely reporting
	Hygiene and sanitation audit scores for CK.


Salary
N80,000 - N100,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@bukkahut.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Quality Control Officer Job at Bukka Hut




        
    

        
        Bukka Hut is a popular Nigerian quick-service restaurant brand known for serving local Nigerian meals in a clean, modern, and comfortable environment. The company started in 2011 and has grown into one of Lagos’ best-known indigenous food chains. The brand combines traditional “buka” style Nigerian food with modern restaurant service and hygiene standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Officer

Job Location: Ogudu, Lagos

Role Summary
     

            
    




	The Quality Control Officer (QC) is responsible for ensuring that all food products prepared in the Central Kitchen and Pastry meet Bukka Hut’s quality, safety, hygiene, and consistency standards before dispatch to outlets.
	They support operational excellence by monitoring processes, validating CCPs, enforcing SOP compliance, and preventing quality defects.


Core Duties 


	Inspect all incoming raw materials for quality, freshness, packaging integrity, and compliance according to SOP.
	Verify supplier documents (COA, shelf life, specifications).
	Monitor all production stages to ensure SOP and CCP compliance.
	Check recipe measurements, cooking times, temperatures, and portion accuracy.
	Ensure proper hygiene practices for staff, equipment, utensils, and workstations.
	Enforce food safety standards and HACCP guidelines.
	Conduct organoleptic analysis ( taste, texture, colour, and appearance) checks on finished products
	Approve or reject finished batches before dispatch.
	Verify packaging, labelling, batch coding, and storage conditions.
	Maintain accurate quality logs, records, and CCP documentation.
	Report non-conformances and follow up on corrective actions.
	Check equipment calibration (thermometers, weighing scales, etc).
	Ensure proper sanitation, cleaning schedules, and waste management.
	Support and train CK staff on hygiene and quality requirements.
	Identify and escalate quality risks or deviations immediately.
	Recommend process or recipe improvements where needed.
	Monitor pest control and environmental conditions.
	Validate dispatch conditions (temperature, packaging, cold chain).
	Ensure only approved products are released to outlets.
	Support audits, internal inspections, and quality reviews.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Reduction in non-conforming materials
	First-time quality pass rate
	Compliance with HACCP and SOP requirements
	Reduction in outlet complaints linked to CK
	Documentation accuracy and timely reporting
	Hygiene and sanitation audit scores for CK.


Salary
N80,000 - N100,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations & Logistics Manager",
  "description": "Waylc Hub - We help you hire vetted and competent Talents, you are guaranteed seamless hiring, fast onboarding, and dedicated support.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations &amp; Logistics Manager

Job Location: Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time (Hybrid)

Job Summary


	We are looking for a proactive and highly organized Operations &amp; Logistics Manager to oversee inventory, logistics, order fulfillment, and operational processes.
	The ideal candidate will ensure products are available, orders are processed efficiently, and deliveries are completed on time while maintaining accurate records and reports.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage and enforce operational SOPs to ensure efficiency and consistency.
	Monitor inventory levels and maintain accurate stock records.
	Confirm customer orders and ensure products are packed and ready for dispatch.
	Coordinate with logistics partners to ensure timely deliveries across Nigeria and other African markets.
	Track shipments and resolve delivery issues when necessary.
	Prepare regular operational, inventory, and performance reports.
	Use inventory management software and digital tools effectively.
	Identify and implement process improvements to support business growth.
	Capture basic behind-the-scenes (BTS) photos and videos when required.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s degree or equivalent experience in Operations, Logistics, Supply Chain, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4 - 6 years of experience in operations, logistics, inventory management, or a similar role.
	Experience using inventory management systems and reporting tools.
	Strong communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work independently with little or no supervision.
	Tech-savvy with proficiency in Microsoft Office and inventory software.
	Strong attention to detail and accountability.


Preferred:


	Experience in FMCG, food products, healthy snacks, or consumer goods.
	Experience managing logistics and distribution networks.
	Self-motivated, reliable, and results-oriented.


Compensation
Salary: N150,000 &ndash; N200,000 per month (depending on experience and qualifications).

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates CV and a brief cover letter outlining their relevant experience in operations, inventory management, and logistics to: hr@waylc.org using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Operations & Logistics Manager Job at Waylc Hub




        
    

        
        Waylc Hub - We help you hire vetted and competent Talents, you are guaranteed seamless hiring, fast onboarding, and dedicated support.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations & Logistics Manager

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time (Hybrid)

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are looking for a proactive and highly organized Operations & Logistics Manager to oversee inventory, logistics, order fulfillment, and operational processes.
	The ideal candidate will ensure products are available, orders are processed efficiently, and deliveries are completed on time while maintaining accurate records and reports.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage and enforce operational SOPs to ensure efficiency and consistency.
	Monitor inventory levels and maintain accurate stock records.
	Confirm customer orders and ensure products are packed and ready for dispatch.
	Coordinate with logistics partners to ensure timely deliveries across Nigeria and other African markets.
	Track shipments and resolve delivery issues when necessary.
	Prepare regular operational, inventory, and performance reports.
	Use inventory management software and digital tools effectively.
	Identify and implement process improvements to support business growth.
	Capture basic behind-the-scenes (BTS) photos and videos when required.


Requirements


	Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience in Operations, Logistics, Supply Chain, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 4 - 6 years of experience in operations, logistics, inventory management, or a similar role.
	Experience using inventory management systems and reporting tools.
	Strong communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work independently with little or no supervision.
	Tech-savvy with proficiency in Microsoft Office and inventory software.
	Strong attention to detail and accountability.


Preferred:


	Experience in FMCG, food products, healthy snacks, or consumer goods.
	Experience managing logistics and distribution networks.
	Self-motivated, reliable, and results-oriented.


Compensation
Salary: N150,000 – N200,000 per month (depending on experience and qualifications).

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates CV and a brief cover letter outlining their relevant experience in operations, inventory management, and logistics to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Officer",
  "description": "Lopterra Services Limited - Our client is an engineering project company with expertise in renewable energy, Power Electronic, HVAC and Electrical services delivering bespoke solutions across various sectors of Nigeria and West Africa Sub-Region, Covering Telecommunication, Hospitality, Transportation, Real Estate, Agriculture, Oil and Gas, Health Care and Government Infrastructure.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented HR officer to join our our organization.
	As an HR officer, you will be an integral part of our team, providing comprehensive HR support and services to our company.
	You will play a critical role in supporting our Human Resources department in various administrative tasks, functions and contribute to the overall efficiency of the HR department.


Key Responsibilites
Human Resources:


	Review and implement HR policies and procedures in line with company goals and labour laws.
	Manage end-to-end recruitment, selection, and onboarding processes.
	Coordinate the biannual performance appraisal and support performance improvement initiatives.
	Conduct Training Needs Analysis (TNA) and implement staff training and development programs.
	Handle employee relations, grievances, and disciplinary processes professionally and confidentially.
	Maintain and update employee records, contracts, and HR database (manual and electronic).
	Support payroll and benefits administration, ensuring compliance with statutory remittances (NSITF, ITF, PENCOM, etc.).
	Organize employee engagement and wellness activities to foster collaboration and inclusion.


Administrative Support:


	Provide administrative assistance to management and departmental heads.
	Assist in ensuring efficient office operations, procurement of office supplies, and vendor coordination.
	Prepare and organize company documentation for client audits, certifications, and renewals.
	Assist in planning company meetings, workshops, and trainings.


Bidding &amp; Compliance Support:


	Assist in preparing tender and bid submissions for private, governmental, and international organizations (e.g., UN, NGOs).
	Ensure timely renewal and documentation of statutory and company certifications required for eligibility in bids.
	Support the collation of prequalification documents, staff CVs, and technical submissions for tender applications.
	Coordinate with relevant departments to ensure compliance with bid requirements and deadlines


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field.
	2&ndash; 3 years of hands-on HR experience, preferably in a project-based or technical environment.
	Certification with the Chartered Institute of Personnel Management (CIPM) or equivalent is an advantage.
	Solid understanding of Nigerian labour laws, HR best practices, and administrative management.
	Experience supporting bid or tender documentation is a plus.
	Excellent communication, organizational, and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and HRIS tools.
	Demonstrated integrity, attention to detail, and ability to multitask under minimal supervision.


Why work with us


	Salary: N150,000 - N180,000 monthly (net)
	Culture of Excellence: Our company fosters a culture of excellence where employees are encouraged to innovate, collaborate, and strive for continuous improvement.
	Opportunities for Growth:joining our team atmeans gaining access to a wealth of opportunities forprofessional growth and advancementwithin the rapidly expanding renewable energy sector.
	As part of our dynamic organization, you&rsquo;ll work with a diverse portfolio of clients across various industries from manufacturing and real estate to agriculture and technology all striving to make the transition to sustainable energy solutions.
	Strong Purpose and Values:Our company is driven by a strong sense of purpose and a set of values that guide everything we do. We are passionate about making a difference in the world and are committed to conducting business ethically, responsibly, and sustainably.
	Strong Community: Our workplace is also our professional family. We look out for our team.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-26",

Human Resources Officer Job at Lopterra Services Limited




        
    

        
        Lopterra Services Limited - Our client is an engineering project company with expertise in renewable energy, Power Electronic, HVAC and Electrical services delivering bespoke solutions across various sectors of Nigeria and West Africa Sub-Region, Covering Telecommunication, Hospitality, Transportation, Real Estate, Agriculture, Oil and Gas, Health Care and Government Infrastructure.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resources Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented HR officer to join our our organization.
	As an HR officer, you will be an integral part of our team, providing comprehensive HR support and services to our company.
	You will play a critical role in supporting our Human Resources department in various administrative tasks, functions and contribute to the overall efficiency of the HR department.


Key Responsibilites
Human Resources:


	Review and implement HR policies and procedures in line with company goals and labour laws.
	Manage end-to-end recruitment, selection, and onboarding processes.
	Coordinate the biannual performance appraisal and support performance improvement initiatives.
	Conduct Training Needs Analysis (TNA) and implement staff training and development programs.
	Handle employee relations, grievances, and disciplinary processes professionally and confidentially.
	Maintain and update employee records, contracts, and HR database (manual and electronic).
	Support payroll and benefits administration, ensuring compliance with statutory remittances (NSITF, ITF, PENCOM, etc.).
	Organize employee engagement and wellness activities to foster collaboration and inclusion.


Administrative Support:


	Provide administrative assistance to management and departmental heads.
	Assist in ensuring efficient office operations, procurement of office supplies, and vendor coordination.
	Prepare and organize company documentation for client audits, certifications, and renewals.
	Assist in planning company meetings, workshops, and trainings.


Bidding & Compliance Support:


	Assist in preparing tender and bid submissions for private, governmental, and international organizations (e.g., UN, NGOs).
	Ensure timely renewal and documentation of statutory and company certifications required for eligibility in bids.
	Support the collation of prequalification documents, staff CVs, and technical submissions for tender applications.
	Coordinate with relevant departments to ensure compliance with bid requirements and deadlines


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field.
	2– 3 years of hands-on HR experience, preferably in a project-based or technical environment.
	Certification with the Chartered Institute of Personnel Management (CIPM) or equivalent is an advantage.
	Solid understanding of Nigerian labour laws, HR best practices, and administrative management.
	Experience supporting bid or tender documentation is a plus.
	Excellent communication, organizational, and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and HRIS tools.
	Demonstrated integrity, attention to detail, and ability to multitask under minimal supervision.


Why work with us


	Salary: N150,000 - N180,000 monthly (net)
	Culture of Excellence: Our company fosters a culture of excellence where employees are encouraged to innovate, collaborate, and strive for continuous improvement.
	Opportunities for Growth:joining our team atmeans gaining access to a wealth of opportunities forprofessional growth and advancementwithin the rapidly expanding renewable energy sector.
	As part of our dynamic organization, you’ll work with a diverse portfolio of clients across various industries from manufacturing and real estate to agriculture and technology all striving to make the transition to sustainable energy solutions.
	Strong Purpose and Values:Our company is driven by a strong sense of purpose and a set of values that guide everything we do. We are passionate about making a difference in the world and are committed to conducting business ethically, responsibly, and sustainably.
	Strong Community: Our workplace is also our professional family. We look out for our team.
